title: Sour Chewy Japanese Chicken Curry
description: A tangy, chewy curry featuring bone‑in, skin‑on chicken legs simmered in a coconut‑miso sauce with a splash of citrus for a bright finish.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat. Add cumin seeds and curry leaves; let sizzle until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Add the remaining tablespoon of olive oil, minced garlic, and diced onion. Sauté until the onion becomes translucent, about 5–7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the ground ginger and a pinch of salt; cook for 1 minute so the flavors release.
Brown the chicken legs on all sides, turning occasionally, for about 8–10 minutes.
Pour in the tomato puree, stir, and let it cook for 2 minutes until the tomato oils separate.
Add the coconut milk, bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 20–25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.
Whisk the miso paste with lemon juice in a small bowl until smooth. Add this mixture to the pot, stirring to combine fully.
Let the curry simmer uncovered for another 10 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken and flavors to meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Serve hot over steamed rice or with a side of seasoned spinach. Garnish with chopped cilantro if desired.
